g.desc: The outline of a rectangular wooden panel. The piece is taller than it is wide, featuring two distinct T-shaped tabs protruding from the top corners and two similar tabs at the bottom corners. At the top center, there is a wide, shallow U-shaped dip in the edge, and directly below that dip is a horizontal, pill-shaped cutout serving as a handle or slot. The vertical sides of the piece have a slight inward taper starting near the top.
g.title: Back (1) - Wood

g.desc: A large, black rubber O-ring. It is a seamless, flexible loop made of smooth synthetic rubber. The overall diameter is approximately 2 inches, roughly the width of a standard credit card. Unlike a flat washer, the ring itself is cylindrical if cut in half; it feels like a thick, continuous noodle joined into a perfect circle.
g.title: O-Ring (1) - Rubber

g.desc: A wide, rectangular wooden panel with rounded corners. A plastic breadboard is mounted to this wood, with a small Raspberry Pi Pico microcontroller board plugged into one end of it. In the center of both the top and bottom long edges, there is a wide, square-shaped tab protruding outward.
g.title: Lid with Breadboard (1) - Wood and Plastic

g.desc: A small, rectangular ultrasonic sensor module. The front features two prominent silver metal cylinders (the "eyes"). On the back of the circuit board, there is a white plastic "Grove" connector housing. This housing is a small, hollow rectangular box with four thin metal pins protected inside. One side of the plastic housing has two raised vertical ribs (the "key"), ensuring the cable can only be plugged in when oriented correctly.
g.title: Ultrasonic Range Finder (1) - Electronic Module

g.desc: A rectangular wooden piece. Each side of the piece has unique features: the left and right sides each have two large rectangular tabs with a small, T-shaped cutout (nut slot) centered between them. The bottom edge has two wide, square tabs. The top edge is flat. The overall shape is symmetrical horizontally.
g.title: Front (1) - Wood

g.desc: A long, irregular side panel. There are two of these pieces that are mirrors of each other. The top edge features a deep, horizontal slot resembling a hook pointing toward the right. The left side is taller, with a rounded protrusion at the top and a keyhole-shaped cutout at the bottom corner. Two large rectangular windows are cut into the center. The smooth side of the wood contains the etched graphics (a robot and a mouse) and must face outward during assembly.
g.title: Side (2) - Wood

g.desc: A small brass screw. The head is a "pan head" style—rounded on top with a flat underside. It features a Phillips-head drive (a cross-shaped indentation). The shaft is 1/2 inch long and fully threaded with #4-40 machine threading, which feels like 40 very fine, closely spaced ridges per inch. The end of the screw is flat rather than pointed.
g.title: 1/2-inch #4-40 Screw (8) - Brass Metal

g.desc: A small, brass metal hex nut. It is a thick, hexagonal (six-sided) ring with a circular hole in the center. The six flat outer faces meet at distinct, slightly rounded corners, and the interior of the central hole is threaded with fine grooves to match a machine screw. Both the top and bottom surfaces are flat.
g.title: #4-40 Hex Nut (8) - Brass Metal

g.desc: A small wooden bracket. The top edge is deeply carved into a wide "U" or "cradle" shape, with a small rounded hill or "peak" rising in the center of the curve. The left and right sides each have a single T-shaped tab pointing outward. The bottom edge is flat but features two small, upright T-shaped cutouts (nut slots) and a single rectangular tab protruding from the center.
g.title: Range Finder Holder (1) - Wood

g.desc: A long, rectangular base panel. One end is flat (the back), while the opposite end (the front) tapers into a rounded, shield-like point. Each long side features three wide, rectangular tabs. Inset from these tabs are four small, T-shaped nut slots—two near the back and two near the middle. The rounded front end contains two vertical rectangular cutouts flanking a central square hole and two small circular holes.
g.title: Bottom (1) - Wood
